auth_notes: Most providers (DoseSpot, DrFirst) require a multi-step authentication process involving a Single Sign-On (SSO) token generation or HMAC signatures. Requires HIPAA-compliant credential storage.
rate_limits: Varies by provider; typically 10-50 requests per second with strict concurrency limits.
gotchas: EPCS (Electronic Prescribing of Controlled Substances) requires additional identity proofing and cannot be fully automated via API alone.
Many providers require the use of an embedded iframe/widget for the actual prescription entry to maintain certification.
Strict BAA (Business Associate Agreement) and HIPAA compliance audits are usually required before production access.
research_notes: Integration must be handled via the HTTP Request node. Because e-prescribing is heavily regulated, the n8n workflow will likely focus on 'Pre-flight' data prep (syncing patient demographics) and 'Post-flight' data handling (receiving webhooks when a prescription is filled) rather than the act of prescribing itself, which usually requires a certified UI.
